game_name: Glory of Heracles IV: Gift from the Gods

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core narrative conceit - player controls young man whose spirit is separated from body
Inherited from Heracles II; fundamental to combat and exploration
Time-flow system affecting NPC availability and story progression
Progressive introduction of mythological locations and adversaries
Narrative climax element; high-stakes mythological consequence

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dramatic narrative-driven pacing with mythological episode progression; builds toward climactic Pandora's box revelation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-level introduction to spirit mechanic and party system
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating mythological encounters with increasing party complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Climactic encounters related to Atlantis and Pandora's box with high narrative stakes

notable_level_patterns.description: Game loosely based on various Greek mythology episodes, progressively introducing classical scenarios
Time-flow system gates access to certain NPCs and story beats; inherited from Heracles II
Core mechanic where protagonist's separated spirit status creates unique interaction possibilities unavailable to normal characters
